proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_042=1 have longer pfs_months than those with feature_042=0 (positive effect of feature_042 on PFS).
Patients with feature_056=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_056=0 (negative effect of feature_056 on PFS).
Patients with feature_048=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_048=0 (negative effect of feature_048 on PFS).
Patients with feature_111=1 have longer pfs_months than those with feature_111=0 (positive effect of feature_111 on PFS).

analyses.result_summary: feature_042: mean PFS 5.408 (n=17428) vs 4.310 (n=32572); diff=+1.098 mo, Welch t-test p=0.000e+00.
feature_056: mean PFS 3.617 (n=14969) vs 5.153 (n=35031); diff=-1.536 mo, Welch t-test p=0.000e+00.
feature_048: mean PFS 3.750 (n=5090) vs 4.800 (n=44910); diff=-1.050 mo, Welch t-test p=1.455e-178.
feature_111: mean PFS 4.865 (n=34886) vs 4.297 (n=15114); diff=+0.568 mo, Welch t-test p=3.705e-143.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063 (ordinal 0-2) shows a negative monotonic relationship with pfs_months: each unit increase shortens PFS.
Patients with feature_015=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_015=0.
Patients with feature_040=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_040=0.
Patients with feature_039=1 have longer pfs_months than those with feature_039=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_015: mean PFS 4.328 (n=17365) vs 4.887 (n=32635); diff=-0.559 mo, Welch t-test p=6.066e-144.
feature_040: mean PFS 4.317 (n=9060) vs 4.776 (n=40940); diff=-0.459 mo, Welch t-test p=6.102e-70.
feature_039: mean PFS 4.824 (n=31954) vs 4.462 (n=18046); diff=+0.362 mo, Welch t-test p=5.823e-59.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 (continuous 30-90, plausibly age) is positively correlated with pfs_months: as feature_080 increases, PFS increases.
feature_019 (continuous 1.5-5.5, plausibly albumin) is positively associated with pfs_months.
feature_067 (continuous 0-24.6) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_101 (continuous 1-100, mean 15.5) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: feature_080: Spearman rho=+0.726 (p=0.000e+00); OLS slope=+0.1759 mo per unit (p=0.000e+00).
feature_019: Spearman rho=+0.102 (p=1.396e-115); OLS slope=+0.4988 mo per unit (p=9.394e-112).
feature_067: Spearman rho=-0.116 (p=4.769e-149); OLS slope=-0.0747 mo per unit (p=4.075e-148).
feature_101: Spearman rho=-0.083 (p=8.056e-77); OLS slope=-0.0188 mo per unit (p=2.979e-93).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months increases monotonically across deciles of feature_080 (no inverted-U shape).
A quadratic term in feature_080 significantly improves the model versus a linear-only model.

analyses.code: ols(pfs ~ f80) vs ols(pfs ~ f80 + f80^2); LR test
analyses.result_summary: Quadratic coef = +0.00060, p=6.238e-25. LR test p=6.215e-25. Linear R2=0.492, quadratic R2=0.493.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across racial groups in feature_011 (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance groups in feature_089 (private, medicare, medicaid, uninsured).

In a multivariable OLS model containing feature_080, feature_063, feature_042, feature_056, feature_048, feature_111, feature_015, feature_040, feature_039, feature_019, feature_067, and feature_101, all twelve features remain individually significant (p<0.05) with the same direction of effect as in their univariate tests.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_042 + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_111 + feature_015 + feature_040 + feature_039 + feature_019 + feature_067 + feature_101', data=df).fit()

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a significant interaction between feature_042 and feature_056 on pfs_months: the protective effect of feature_042 differs by feature_056 status.

The slope of feature_080 on pfs_months differs by feature_042 status (significant feature_080 x feature_042 interaction).
The slope of feature_080 on pfs_months differs by feature_063 level (significant feature_080 x feature_063 interaction).
The protective effect of feature_042 on pfs_months differs by race (feature_011): testing feature_042 x feature_011 interaction.
The harmful effect of feature_056 on pfs_months differs by race (feature_011 interaction).

analyses.code: ols(pfs ~ f042) vs ols(pfs ~ f042 + f080)
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted f042 beta = +1.098 (p=0.00e+00). Adjusted for f080: beta = +1.092 (p=0.00e+00).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the strong predictors (feature_080, feature_063, feature_042, feature_056), pfs_months still differs across racial groups (feature_011) - i.e., a residual disparity exists.
After adjusting for the strong predictors, pfs_months still differs across insurance groups (feature_089).
Distributions of feature_042 (treatment-like) differ by race (feature_011) -- chi-square test of independence.
Distributions of feature_042 (treatment-like) differ by insurance (feature_089).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with pfs_months > 10 mo, feature_080 distribution is concentrated at the high end (>= 75); among patients with pfs_months < 2, feature_080 is concentrated at the low end.
feature_042 prevalence is significantly higher among long-PFS (>10mo) than short-PFS (<2mo) patients.
feature_056 prevalence is significantly higher among short-PFS (<2mo) than long-PFS (>10mo) patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_080 in long-PFS (>10mo, n=1554) = 75.46; short-PFS (<2mo, n=6688) = 52.23. Diff=+23.23, p=0.000e+00.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_086 (binary, ~15%) is associated with pfs_months in the screening direction (negative).
feature_077 (binary, ~35%) is positively associated with pfs_months but the effect is small (<0.1 mo).
feature_031 (binary, ~20%) is negatively associated with pfs_months (small).
feature_005 (binary, ~30%) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_034 (binary, ~37%) is positively associated with pfs_months (small).
Within elderly patients (feature_080 >= 75), the protective effect of feature_042 is preserved (>= 0.5 mo).
Within young patients (feature_080 < 50), the protective effect of feature_042 is preserved (>= 0.5 mo).
Within elderly patients (feature_080 >= 75), the harmful effect of feature_056 is preserved.
Within young patients (feature_080 < 50), the harmful effect of feature_056 is preserved.

After conditioning on the 12 strong predictors, the residuals show no remaining association with insurance feature_089.

analyses.result_summary: Residual ANOVA across insurance: F=0.204, p=8.935e-01. Mean residual by insurance: {'medicaid': -0.003602407020418514, 'private': -0.0032834858873790175, 'medicare': 0.0032108897819401417, 'uninsured': 0.011526731570972473}.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS model containing all 127 features (with one-hot encoding for the 2 categoricals) achieves an out-of-sample R^2 of at least 0.70 for predicting pfs_months under 5-fold cross-validation.
feature_080 alone explains at least 50% of pfs_months variance (R^2 >= 0.50).

analyses.code: 5-fold CV OLS via numpy lstsq on all 127 features (one-hot encoded)
analyses.result_summary: Cross-validated out-of-sample R2 (5-fold OLS): mean=0.7914, std=0.0024, folds=[np.float64(0.7874), np.float64(0.7944), np.float64(0.7906), np.float64(0.793), np.float64(0.7919)].
